paper_title: HTLM: Hyper-Text Pre-Training and Prompting of Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces HTLM—a hyper-text pretrained language model using BART-style denoising autoencoding on simplified HTML, incorporating size hints for precise text reconstruction and enabling structured prompting via HTML tag infilling. This leverages end-task-adjacent supervision embedded in HTML tags (e.g., `<title>`), improving data efficiency and zero-shot task generalization
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: 23TB of simplified HTML extracted from Common Crawl (January 2021 snapshot)
raw_extraction.application: Structured prompting for zero-shot and few-shot learning across classification, summarization, and table-to-text generation tasks; auto-prompting via HTML template generation
raw_extraction.license: Not explicitly stated (assumed open-source per Facebook AI practices)
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: BART-style denoising autoencoding with span masking and size hints
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Discriminative fine-tuning for classification tasks and generative fine-tuning for summarization tasks
raw_extraction.hardware_used: 330,000 steps on 256 GPUs with batch size of 8192
